<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

The pull request migrates the application from ORPC (a router-based RPC framework) to TanStack Start server functions with React Query. It removes the entire ORPC infrastructure (`src/server/orpc.ts`, `src/server/router.ts`, and all sub-routers), replacing it with a new `src/server/functions/` directory containing domain-specific handlers, server functions, and a centralized middleware layer. Client code switches from `orpc.*` calls to query/mutation helpers exported from `src/server/functions/queries.ts`. A new `ServerFnError` class standardizes error handling across server procedures. The API documentation routes (`/api/openapi/*` and `/api/rest/$`, `/api/rpc/$`) are removed. Sessions are updated to exclude `token` and include `ipAddress` and `userAgent` fields. The migration maintains the same CRUD operations and business logic while restructuring how requests are processed and validated.

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the application's API layer by replacing oRPC with TanStack Start Server Functions, which includes a complete overhaul of the backend handlers, middleware, and frontend data fetching logic. It also introduces a custom error handling system and provides comprehensive deployment documentation. Feedback highlights critical typos in Zod schemas where a non-existent .prefault() method was used instead of .default(). Additionally, security concerns were raised regarding insufficient permission checks for user updates and a logic bug in the email verification process that could lead to unauthorized data changes or incorrect verification states.

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The method `.prefault()` is not a standard Zod method and will cause a runtime error. It appears to be a typo for `.default()`. This occurs in multiple places in this file and others.

```suggestion
      limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20),
      searchTerm: z.string().trim().optional().default(''),
    })
    .default({});
```

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The method `.prefault()` is not a standard Zod method. Please use `.default()` instead.

```suggestion
      limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20),
      searchTerm: z.string().optional(),
    })
    .default({});
```

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The method `.prefault()` is not a standard Zod method. Please use `.default()` instead.

```suggestion
      limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20),
      searchTerm: z.string().trim().optional().default(''),
    })
    .default({});
```

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The method `.prefault()` is not a standard Zod method. Please use `.default()` instead.

```suggestion
    limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20),
```

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The permission check `user: ['set-role']` is too narrow for a handler that updates the user's name and email. A user with permission to change roles but not personal info could exploit this to modify other users' data. Consider using a more appropriate permission like `user: ['update']`.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There is a logic bug here: if `data.email` is `undefined` (not provided in the update), `currentUser.email !== data.email` will be `true`, causing `emailVerified` to be set to `true` even though the email wasn't changed. Additionally, automatically marking a new email as verified is a security risk; it should typically be set to `false` until verified by the user.

```suggestion
      emailVerified: (data.email !== undefined && currentUser.email !== data.email) ? false : undefined,
```

**Actionable comments posted: 5**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@README.md`:
- Line 171: The README currently hardcodes PNPM_VERSION=10.33.0 which can drift
from the packageManager in package.json; update the README to instruct users to
use the pnpm version from the packageManager field in package.json (or derive it
automatically), e.g., replace the hardcoded `PNPM_VERSION=10.33.0` note with
guidance to read `package.json`'s `packageManager` value or run a script to
extract it so CI and local builds match the `packageManager` declaration.
- Line 154: The README currently lists "Run `pnpm db:push` or your migration
command against the production database" which encourages schema pushes that
bypass migration history; update the sentence to prioritize migrations by
removing or de-emphasizing `pnpm db:push` and instead instruct: run your
migration command (e.g., the project's migration tool) against the production
database before serving traffic, explicitly avoiding `pnpm db:push` in
production to prevent schema drift. Ensure the updated wording refers to "your
migration command" as the primary action and mentions that `pnpm db:push` should
not be used for production deployments.

In `@src/server/functions/config.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 19-25: The test currently uses expect.any(Boolean) for
result.isDev which is too permissive; change the assertion to assert the exact
expected boolean value for isDev based on the test setup so regressions are
caught. Locate the test that calls handlers.env() and replace the
expect.any(Boolean) entry for isDev with a concrete assertion (e.g., isDev: true
or isDev: false) or compute the expected value from the same environment
variables used by handlers.env() and assert equality (e.g.,
expect(result.isDev).toBe(expectedIsDev)). Ensure the unique symbols referenced
are handlers.env(), result, and isDev.

In `@src/server/functions/user.handlers.server.ts`:
- Around line 234-238: The cursor lookup using cursorRow
(ctx.db.query.session.findFirst) only filters by session.id (data.cursor), which
allows sessions belonging to other users to be used; update the where clause in
that findFirst call to constrain by both session.id equal to data.cursor and
session.userId equal to data.userId (i.e., add an additional equality condition
using session.userId and data.userId) so the cursor is validated against the
target user before pagination proceeds.

In `@src/server/middlewares.server.ts`:
- Around line 125-140: In handleError, ensure the original error is logged
before or alongside the mapped error so unhandled cases don't lose
stack/details: when calling mapDbError(error) in handleError, detect if the
mapping produced a generic/unhandled wrapper (e.g., mappedError.message ===
'Unhandled error' or mappedError !== error) and then call procedureLogger.error
with the original error/stack (the incoming error variable) before
procedureLogger[logLevel](mappedError); keep using ProcedureLogger and preserve
existing logLevel determination and final mappedError return.
```

</details>

**Prioritize migration-based database setup in production deployment steps.**

The current wording lists `pnpm db:push` first, which bypasses migration history and can lead to schema drift. For production deployments, migration-only wording aligns with standard best practices.

<details>
<summary>Suggested doc change</summary>

```diff
-* Run `pnpm db:push` or your migration command against the production database before serving production traffic.
+* Run your versioned migration command against the production database before serving production traffic.
```
</details>

**Keep `PNPM_VERSION` in README aligned with `packageManager` specification in `package.json` to prevent CI mismatch.**

The README hardcodes `PNPM_VERSION=10.33.0` for Cloudflare dashboard builds, but this version can drift from the actual `pnpm@10.33.0` specified in `package.json` when dependencies are upgraded. Consider removing the exact version here and instructing users to match the version from `packageManager` in `package.json`, or use a build script to automatically derive it during documentation generation.

**Scope session cursor lookup to the target user.**

At **Line 236**, cursor lookup only checks `session.id`. A cursor from a different user can be treated as valid and skew pagination for `data.userId`. Constrain the lookup by both session id and user id.

 

<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
   const cursorRow = data.cursor
     ? await ctx.db.query.session.findFirst({
-        where: eq(session.id, data.cursor),
+        where: and(
+          eq(session.id, data.cursor),
+          eq(session.userId, data.userId)
+        ),
         columns: { id: true, createdAt: true },
       })
     : undefined;
```
</details>

_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Consider logging the original error before mapping for unhandled cases.**

When an unhandled error (non-`ServerFnError`, non-`PgError`) is thrown, `mapDbError` wraps it with a generic "Unhandled error" message (line 166-169). The log statement at line 137 then logs the mapped error, losing the original error details and stack trace.

For debugging production issues, consider logging the original error before mapping:



<details>
<summary>♻️ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 const handleError = (error: unknown, procedureLogger: ProcedureLogger) => {
+  // Log original error for debugging before mapping
+  if (!(error instanceof ServerFnError)) {
+    procedureLogger.error({ originalError: error }, 'Unhandled error before mapping');
+  }
   const mappedError = mapDbError(error);
   const logLevel = (() => {
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@src/server/middlewares.server.ts` around lines 125 - 140, In handleError,
ensure the original error is logged before or alongside the mapped error so
unhandled cases don't lose stack/details: when calling mapDbError(error) in
handleError, detect if the mapping produced a generic/unhandled wrapper (e.g.,
mappedError.message === 'Unhandled error' or mappedError !== error) and then
call procedureLogger.error with the original error/stack (the incoming error
variable) before procedureLogger[logLevel](mappedError); keep using
ProcedureLogger and preserve existing logLevel determination and final
mappedError return.
```

</details>

P2: Cursor validation in `getUserSessions` is not scoped to the requested user. A session ID from another user is accepted and can produce incorrect paginated results for `data.userId` instead of being treated as an invalid cursor.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

The permission check `user: ['set-role']` is too narrow for a handler that updates the user's name and email. A user with permission to change roles but not personal info could exploit this to modify other users' data. Consider using a more appropriate permission like `user: ['update']`.

![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There is a logic bug here: if `data.email` is `undefined` (not provided in the update), `currentUser.email !== data.email` will be `true`, causing `emailVerified` to be set to `true` even though the email wasn't changed. Additionally, automatically marking a new email as verified is a security risk; it should typically be set to `false` until verified by the user.

```suggestion
      emailVerified: (data.email !== undefined && currentUser.email !== data.email) ? false : undefined,
```

**Prioritize migration-based database setup in production deployment steps.**

The current wording lists `pnpm db:push` first, which bypasses migration history and can lead to schema drift. For production deployments, migration-only wording aligns with standard best practices.

<summary>Suggested doc change</summary>
